title: AGENTS.md
text: Guidance AI agents (humans) working repository.

title: AGENTS.md
text: gdalviz parses, validates, visualizes modern GDAL CLI pipelines (gdal vector pipeline ! ... command lines GDALG .gdalg.json files). architecture strict one-way flow: R/contract.R loads bundled gdal vector pipeline --json-usage snapshot (inst/extdata/gdal_vector_pipeline_usage.json) — single source truth valid steps/arguments, used parser lint_pipeline(). R/render.R inverse parser (command lines, shell scripts, GDALG output).
code: parse_pipeline() / read_gdalg()   R/parse.R, R/read.R         -> gdalviz_pipeline pipeline_graph()                  R/graph.R (+ R/categories.R)         -> gdalviz_graph          renderer-agnostic nodes/edges tibbles render_reactflow() / render_g6() / render_diagrammer()         -> htmlwidgets            R/render-*.R + srcjs/ (React Flow)

title: AGENTS.md
text: graph model stays renderer-agnostic. Renderer-specific styling lives renderers, never R/graph.R. React Flow bundle inst/htmlwidgets/pipeline_flow.js built srcjs/ committed, package users never need node/bun. srcjs/ change run bun run build (srcjs/) commit bundle. widget payload contract must stay sync across three places: R/render-reactflow.R (serialization), R/graph.R (node fields), srcjs/src/types.ts. contract snapshot regenerated gdalviz_refresh_contract(), edited hand.

title: AGENTS.md
code: # R (from the package root) Rscript -e "pkgload::load_all('.'); testthat::test_dir('tests/testthat')" Rscript -e "roxygen2::roxygenise()" Rscript -e "rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck(args = '--no-manual')" Rscript dev/scripts/build_site.R      # pkgdown (windows temp-drive workaround)  # JS/TS (from srcjs/) bun install bun run typecheck bun run build                          # -> inst/htmlwidgets/pipeline_flow.js bun run watch

title: AGENTS.md
text: R: native pipe |> (never %>%); namespace-prefix non-base calls (cli::cli_abort(), rlang::arg_match()); rlang + cli conditions/validation; httr2 httr; testthat edition 3. Comments: sparse, lowercase, non-obvious logic. Docs: roxygen2 markdown; vignettes/articles Quarto (.qmd), built VignetteBuilder: quarto. Heavy interactive examples belong vignettes/articles/ (pkgdown-, shipped package). Commits: conventional style scope, e.g. feat(pipeline): ..., docs(cicd): ..., fix(parse): ..., chore(meta): .... emojis anywhere.

title: AGENTS.md
text: dev/ .Rbuildignore’d scratch/tooling space; dev/scratch/ gitignored entirely. put anything load-bearing . inst/extdata/pipelines/ ships example pipeline data (GDALG JSON); scripts generate examples live dev/scripts/. inst/schemas/ holds upstream GDAL JSON schemas reference/validation. Test corpus expectations live tests/testthat/; widget bundle guard test (test-widget-assets.R).

title: Getting started with gdalviz
text: parse_pipeline() accepts raw command line, without gdal vector pipeline prefix: read_gdalg() additionally accepts paths .gdalg.json files, raw pipeline text files, pasted multiline bash / PowerShell scripts – line continuations, heredocs, PowerShell \" quoting normalized automatically:
code: cmd <- paste(   "gdal vector pipeline",   "--config GDAL_NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS",   "! read --input parcels.gpkg --input-layer parcels",   "! filter --where \"statefp = '13'\"",   "! make-valid",   "! reproject --output-crs EPSG:4326",   "! write --output parcels.fgb --output-format FlatGeobuf" )  p <- parse_pipeline(cmd) p #> ── <gdalviz_pipeline> ────────────────────────────────── gdal vector pipeline ── #> global: `--config GDAL_NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS` #> 5 steps: #> 1. read `--input parcels.gpkg --input-layer parcels` #> 2. filter `--where "statefp = '13'"` #> 3. make-valid #> 4. reproject `--output-crs EPSG:4326` #> 5. write `--output parcels.fgb --output-format FlatGeobuf` script <- "gdal vector pipeline `   read --input parcels.gpkg ` ! reproject --output-crs 'EPSG:4326' ` ! write --output out.fgb"  read_gdalg(script) #> ── <gdalviz_pipeline> ────────────────────────────────── gdal vector pipeline ── #> 3 steps: #> 1. read `--input parcels.gpkg` #> 2. reproject `--output-crs EPSG:4326` #> 3. write `--output out.fgb`

title: Getting started with gdalviz
text: single source truth valid steps arguments GDAL’s --json-usage output. snapshot bundled package, gdalviz_refresh_contract() regenerates locally installed GDAL. lint_pipeline() returns one row per issue; validate_pipeline() wraps result pass/fail object: Unknown steps arguments, missing required arguments, invalid enum choices, mutually exclusive combinations (like --append + --upsert ) reported.
code: bad <- "read --input in.gpkg ! reproject ! write --output out.fgb --append --upsert" lint_pipeline(bad) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   step_index command   level code                    message                     #>        <int> <chr>     <chr> <chr>                   <chr>                       #> 1          2 reproject error missing_required_args   Missing required argument(… #> 2          3 write     error mutually_exclusive_args Arguments --append, --upse…

title: Getting started with gdalviz
text: pipeline_graph() turns pipeline renderer-agnostic dataflow model: nodes carry semantic category (source, filter, geometry, …), plain-language description, propagated feature-stream state (CRS, geometry type, field schema); edges carry “changed” badges. GDAL nuances get dedicated treatment: pipeline-level options (--config, --progress) group single runtime configuration node feeding source, GDALG pipelines omit terminal write; graph adds implicit streamed output sink flow reads complete, runs 3+ consecutive identical steps (example one-field---time set-field-type chains needed schema overrides) merge one stacked node. Opt merge_repeated = FALSE.
code: g <- pipeline_graph(cmd) g$nodes[, c("command", "category", "description")] #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   command    category description                             #>   <chr>      <chr>    <chr>                                   #> 1 config     runtime  Runtime configuration (1 config option) #> 2 read       source   Read layer 'parcels'                    #> 3 filter     filter   Keep features where statefp = '13'      #> 4 make-valid geometry Repair invalid geometries               #> 5 reproject  crs      Reproject to EPSG:4326                  #> 6 write      sink     Write to parcels.fgb as FlatGeobuf

title: Getting started with gdalviz
text: render_reactflow() produces interactive React Flow widget bundled package (node toolchain required). Drag pan, scroll zoom, click node open inspector step’s arguments, propagated stream state, link GDAL documentation. Layout direction ("TB", "LR", …), light/dark themes, minimap/controls/legend overlays arguments. static output use render_diagrammer() (Graphviz), render_g6() AntV G6 alternative. See pipeline gallery article larger real-world examples (tee branching, config-heavy runs, merged schema chains).
code: render_reactflow(g, theme = "light", height = 460)

title: Getting started with gdalviz
text: Parsed pipelines serialize back losslessly: can formatted ready--run shell scripts either bash PowerShell, optionally reflowing SQL heredocs: as_gdalg() / write_gdalg() produce GDALG JSON specifications directly.
code: render_command_line(p) #> [1] "gdal vector pipeline --config GDAL_NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS ! read --input parcels.gpkg --input-layer parcels ! filter --where \"statefp = '13'\" ! make-valid ! reproject --output-crs EPSG:4326 ! write --output parcels.fgb --output-format FlatGeobuf" cat(render_script(p, shell = "bash")) #> gdal vector pipeline \ #>   --config GDAL_NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS \ #>   ! read --input parcels.gpkg --input-layer parcels \ #>   ! filter --where "statefp = '13'" \ #>   ! make-valid \ #>   ! reproject --output-crs EPSG:4326 \ #>   ! write --output parcels.fgb --output-format FlatGeobuf

what: TIGER states: a linear ETL pipeline
title: Pipeline gallery
text: classic download-filter-reshape-write flow, straight US Census TIGER shapefiles /vsizip/vsicurl/. Note edge badges tracking changes along stream: SQL step reduces schema 6 fields, reproject stamps CRS.
code: tiger <- paste(   "gdal vector pipeline",   '! read --input "/vsizip/vsicurl/https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/TIGER2025/STATE/tl_2025_us_state.zip/tl_2025_us_state.shp"',   '--input-layer tl_2025_us_state --open-option ENCODING=UTF-8 --open-option ADJUST_GEOM_TYPE=FIRST_SHAPE',   "! filter --where \"STATEFP NOT IN ('02','15','60','66','69','72','74','78')\"",   '! sql --sql "SELECT GEOID AS geoid, STATEFP AS state_fips, STUSPS AS state_abbr,',   'NAME AS state_name, ALAND AS area_land_m2, AWATER AS area_water_m2 FROM tl_2025_us_state"',   "! make-valid",   "! set-geom-type --multi --skip",   "! sort --method hilbert",   "! write --output tiger_states.parquet --output-format Parquet",   '--layer-creation-option COMPRESSION=ZSTD --layer-creation-option GEOMETRY_ENCODING=GEOARROW' )  render_reactflow(tiger, theme = "dark", height = 620)

what: Tee branching: valid / invalid geometry triage
title: Pipeline gallery
text: tee fans feature stream side pipelines – splitting parcels ST_IsValid() separate outputs main stream continues Parquet write. Branch flows render dashed.
code: triage <- paste(   "gdal vector pipeline",   '! read --input parcels.gpkg --layer parcels_statefp13',   '! tee',   '--tee-pipeline [ sql --sql "SELECT * FROM parcels_statefp13 WHERE ST_IsValid(geom)" --dialect SQLITE',   '! write --output valid_parcels.gpkg --overwrite ]',   '--tee-pipeline [ sql --sql "SELECT * FROM parcels_statefp13 WHERE NOT ST_IsValid(geom)" --dialect SQLITE',   '! check-geometry ! write --output invalid_parcels.gpkg --overwrite ]',   '! write --output all_parcels.parquet --output-format Parquet',   '--layer-creation-option COMPRESSION=ZSTD' )  render_reactflow(triage, direction = "LR", theme = "light", height = 540)

what: Config-heavy runs and merged schema chains
title: Pipeline gallery
text: production parcels ETL: eight --config options group runtime configuration node, thirty-two one-field---time set-field-type steps (GDAL nuance – command accepts one field per step) merge single stacked set-field-type ×32 node, pipeline writes GDALG, graph attaches implicit streamed output sink. Click merged node see every field -> type coercion.
code: fields <- c(   source_fid = "Integer64", geoid = "String", state_fips = "String",   county_fips = "String", tax_year = "Integer", num_buildings = "Integer",   num_units = "Integer", year_built = "Integer", num_floors = "Integer",   building_sqft = "Integer64", num_bedrooms = "Integer",   num_half_baths = "Integer", num_full_baths = "Integer",   improvement_value = "Integer64", land_value = "Integer64",   ag_value = "Integer64", total_value = "Integer64", tax_acres = "Real",   calc_area = "Real", sale_amount = "Real", sale_date = "Date",   firm_date = "Date", static_bfe = "Real", elevation_min = "Real",   elevation_max = "Real", elevation_avg = "Real", num_fireplaces = "Integer",   updated = "Date", centroid_x = "Real", centroid_y = "Real",   surface_point_x = "Real", surface_point_y = "Real" )  parcels <- paste(   "gdal vector pipeline",   "--progress",   "--config CPL_DEBUG=ON --config CPL_TIMESTAMP=ON --config CPL_LOG_ERRORS=ON",   "--config CPL_LOG=logs/state_fips=44.gdal.log --config GDAL_NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS",   "--config OGR_GPKG_NUM_THREADS=4 --config OGR_SQLITE_CACHE=4000000",   "--config OGR_SQLITE_SYNCHRONOUS=OFF",   "read --input parcels/LR_PARCEL_NATIONWIDE_FILE_US_2026_Q2.gpkg",   "--input-layer lr_parcel_us --open-option LIST_ALL_TABLES=NO",   '! filter --bbox "-71.907258,41.095834,-71.088571,42.018799" --where "statefp=\'44\'"',   "! sql --sql @sql/gpkg.schema.full.sql --dialect SQLITE",   "! make-valid",   "! set-geom-type --multi --skip",   paste(     sprintf("! set-field-type --field-name %s --field-type %s", names(fields), fields),     collapse = " "   ),   "! reproject --output-crs EPSG:4326" )  render_reactflow(parcels, theme = "dark", height = 720)

title: Pipeline gallery
text: parser accepts pasted PowerShell (bash) scripts line continuations – handy pipelines live .ps1 files:
code: ps1 <- 'gdal vector pipeline `   read --input "G:/data/states.gpkg" --input-layer states ` ! filter --where "STUSPS IN (\'GA\',\'FL\',\'AL\')" ` ! reproject --output-crs EPSG:5070 ` ! write --output southeast.fgb --output-format FlatGeobuf --overwrite'  render_reactflow(ps1, direction = "LR", theme = "light", height = 320)

title: Pipeline gallery
text: Every diagram backed parsed model, nothing render-. graph plain data: pipeline serializes back canonical command line, formatted shell script, GDALG specification via render_command_line(), render_script(), as_gdalg().
code: g <- pipeline_graph(triage) g$nodes[, c("command", "category", "branch_role", "description")] #> # A tibble: 8 × 4 #>   command        category  branch_role description                             #>   <chr>          <chr>     <chr>       <chr>                                   #> 1 read           source    main        Read layer 'parcels_statefp13'          #> 2 tee            branch    main        Split stream into 2 side outputs        #> 3 sql            attribute tee         Transform attributes with SQL           #> 4 write          sink      tee         Write to valid_parcels.gpkg             #> 5 sql            attribute tee         Transform attributes with SQL           #> 6 check-geometry inspect   tee         Flag invalid or non-simple geometries   #> 7 write          sink      tee         Write to invalid_parcels.gpkg           #> 8 write          sink      main        Write to all_parcels.parquet as Parquet g$edges #> # A tibble: 7 × 4 #>   from  to    kind  badge #>   <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr> #> 1 n1    n2    main  <NA>  #> 2 n2    n3    main  <NA>  #> 3 n3    n4    main  <NA>  #> 4 n2    n5    main  <NA>  #> 5 n5    n6    main  <NA>  #> 6 n6    n7    main  <NA>  #> 7 n2    n8    main  <NA>

title: Authors and Citation
text: Briggs J (2026). gdalviz: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines. R package version 0.1.0, http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalviz/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {gdalviz: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines},   author = {Jimmy Briggs},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalviz/}, }

title: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines
text: gdalviz parses, validates, visualizes modern GDAL CLI pipelines (gdal vector pipeline ! ... command lines GDALG .gdalg.json files), turning diagrams read like happens data rather shell syntax. Contract-driven - steps arguments validated GDAL’s --json-usage metadata (bundled snapshot, refreshable installed GDAL via gdalviz_refresh_contract()), including required arguments, enum choices, mutually exclusive groups. Flexible input - raw pipeline strings, .gdalg.json files, pasted bash / PowerShell scripts (line continuations, heredocs, \" quoting normalized automatically). Semantic graphs - pipeline_graph() builds renderer-agnostic dataflow model: category classification, feature-stream state propagation (CRS, geometry type, field schema), runtime --config grouping, GDALG-omitted write step explicit streamed sink, merging long repeated-step runs (e.g. one-field---time set-field-type chains). Renderers - interactive React Flow (render_reactflow(), bundled - node toolchain needed), static Graphviz (render_diagrammer()), AntV G6 (render_g6()). Round-trip - serialize back canonical command lines (render_command_line()), formatted bash / PowerShell scripts (render_script()), GDALG JSON (as_gdalg() / write_gdalg()).

title: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines
code: # from r-universe (binaries) install.packages("gdalviz", repos = c("https://jimbrig.r-universe.dev", "https://cloud.r-project.org"))  # or from github pak::pak("jimbrig/gdalviz")

title: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines
text: Parse, lint, render pipeline: interactive React Flow renderer adds pan/zoom, minimap, click--open inspector step’s arguments, propagated stream state, GDAL docs link. GDALG files work directly - note implicit streamed output sink attached GDALG omits final write: Pasted shell scripts (bash PowerShell) parse -, everything round-trips back :
code: library(gdalviz)  cmd <- paste(   "gdal vector pipeline",   "read --input /data/parcels.gpkg --input-layer parcels",   "! filter --where \"statefp = '13'\"",   "! make-valid",   "! reproject --output-crs EPSG:4326",   "! write --output /tmp/parcels.fgb --output-format FlatGeobuf" )  p <- parse_pipeline(cmd) lint_pipeline(p)       # one row per contract violation (none here)  g <- pipeline_graph(p) # renderer-agnostic dataflow model render_diagrammer(g)   # static graphviz rendering system.file("extdata", "pipelines", "tiger_states.gdalg.json", package = "gdalviz") |>   pipeline_graph() |>   render_reactflow(theme = "dark", minimap = FALSE, direction = "TB") render_command_line(p)             # canonical single-line command cat(render_script(p, shell = "bash"))  # formatted multi-line script write_gdalg(p, "parcels.gdalg.json")   # GDALG specification

what: Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
title: Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
text: Converts parse_pipeline() result graph nodes edges, classifying step, rendering arguments code, generating plain-language description, propagating feature-stream state (CRS, geometry type, field schema, validity, ordering) along pipeline.

title: Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
code: pipeline_graph(x, contract = gdalviz_contract(), merge_repeated = TRUE)

title: Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
text: x gdalviz_pipeline (parse_pipeline()) string/path accepted read_gdalg(). contract gdalviz_contract. merge_repeated Merge runs 3+ consecutive identical commands (e.g. one-field---time set-field-type chains needed schema overrides) single stacked node. Defaults TRUE.

title: Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
text: gdalviz_graph: list nodes edges tibbles.

what: Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
title: Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
text: Serializes parsed parse_pipeline() result back single-line command_line string, using minimal quoting GDAL uses writes *.gdalg.json files. result round-trips: parsing output yields equivalent pipeline.

title: Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
code: render_command_line(x, prog = TRUE, type = NULL)

title: Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
text: x gdalviz_pipeline, string/path accepted read_gdalg(). prog Logical NULL. Whether emit leading gdal <type> pipeline program prefix. Defaults TRUE. type Pipeline type ("vector" "raster"). Defaults type detected parsing, falling back "vector".

title: Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
text: length-one character vector.

what: Render a pipeline graph as a Graphviz diagram (static-capable) — render_diagrammer
title: Render a pipeline graph as a Graphviz diagram (static-capable) — render_diagrammer
text: Builds DOT specification card-style HTML nodes (verb header, code body, plain-language description), category colors, state-annotated edges, rendered via DiagrammeR. Suitable static export SVG/PNG.

text: graph gdalviz_graph pipeline_graph(). direction Layout direction: "TB" (vertical, default) "LR". theme "light" "dark".

what: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
text: Produces modern, interactive node-link diagram using dagre (hierarchical) layout. Nodes card-style, colored category, labelled step verb plain-language description; edges carry state-change badges.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
code: render_g6(   graph,   direction = c("TB", "LR", "BT", "RL"),   theme = c("light", "dark"),   height = NULL,   width = "100%" )

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
text: graph gdalviz_graph pipeline_graph(). direction Layout direction: "TB" (default), "LR", "BT", "RL". theme "light" "dark". height, width Widget dimensions.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
text: g6 htmlwidget.

what: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
text: Produces modern, interactive dataflow diagram GDAL pipeline using React Flow (xyflow). Steps render card-style nodes colored category arguments inline; edges carry state-change badges (CRS, geometry type, field count); tee branches render dashed side flows. Clicking node opens inspector panel full argument list, propagated stream state, link GDAL documentation step.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
code: render_reactflow(   graph,   direction = c("TB", "LR", "BT", "RL"),   theme = c("light", "dark"),   minimap = TRUE,   controls = TRUE,   legend = TRUE,   draggable = TRUE,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   elementId = NULL )

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
text: graph gdalviz_graph pipeline_graph(), pipeline/string/path accepted . direction Layout direction: "TB" (default), "LR", "BT", "RL". theme "light" (default) "dark". minimap Show minimap overview. Defaults TRUE. controls Show zoom/fit controls. Defaults TRUE. legend Show category legend. Defaults TRUE. draggable Allow nodes repositioned dragging. Defaults TRUE. width, height Widget dimensions passed htmlwidgets::createWidget(). elementId Optional explicit element id widget container.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
text: pipeline_flow htmlwidget.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
text: JavaScript bundle built srcjs/ (see srcjs/README.md) shipped package, node toolchain needed run time.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gdalg <- system.file(   "extdata", "pipelines", "tiger_states.gdalg.json",   package = "gdalviz" ) pipeline_graph(gdalg) |> render_reactflow(theme = "dark") } # }

what: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
title: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
text: Produces indented, line-continued gdal <type> pipeline invocation either bash/sh powershell, one ! step per line shell-appropriate quoting. Optionally reflows large SQL heredoc (bash) -string (powershell) readability.

title: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
code: render_script(   x,   shell = c("bash", "powershell"),   indent = "  ",   type = NULL,   globals_per_line = 3L,   sql = c("inline", "block") )

title: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
text: x gdalviz_pipeline, string/path accepted read_gdalg(). shell Target shell: "bash" (default) "powershell". indent Indentation unit steps. Defaults two spaces. type Pipeline type ("vector" "raster"). globals_per_line Number global options place per continuation line. Defaults 3. sql Either "inline" (default, single quoted token) "block" (multiline heredoc / -string --sql values).

title: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
text: length-one character vector containing full script.

title: gdalviz 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: gdalviz 0.1.0
text: parse_pipeline() parses valid gdal vector pipeline command line: step splitting (!), nested pipelines ([ ... ], --tee-pipeline), flag/value/positional/short argument forms, quoting, @file arguments. read_gdalg() accepts raw pipeline strings, GDALG (.gdalg.json) files, pipeline text files, pasted multiline bash / PowerShell scripts (continuations, heredocs/-strings, PowerShell \" quoting normalized automatically). parse_script() / read_script() handle explicit script input. Pipeline-level options (--config, --progress) parse pipeline_options, including first step follows without ! separator. lint_pipeline() / validate_pipeline() check pipelines GDAL’s --json-usage contract: unknown steps/arguments, missing required arguments, boolean/value mismatches, invalid enum choices, mutually exclusive argument groups (mutual_exclusion_group). gdalviz_contract() loads bundled contract snapshot (steps, argument metadata, pipeline-level arguments); gdalviz_refresh_contract() regenerates snapshot installed GDAL CLI.

title: gdalviz 0.1.0
text: pipeline_graph() builds renderer-agnostic dataflow graph: semantic categories, plain-language descriptions, structured arguments, docs URLs, feature-stream state propagation (CRS, geometry type, fields, validity, ordering) “changed” edge badges. Runtime configuration renders dedicated node feeding source; GDALG pipelines omitted write gain implicit streamed sink; runs 3+ consecutive identical steps merge one stacked node (merge_repeated = TRUE).

title: gdalviz 0.1.0
text: render_reactflow(): interactive React Flow htmlwidget (bundled, node toolchain needed) card-style nodes, dagre layout, minimap, controls, category legend, light/dark themes, click--open inspector showing step code, arguments, stream state, GDAL docs links. Shiny bindings via pipelineFlowOutput() / renderPipelineFlow(). render_diagrammer() / pipeline_dot(): static-capable Graphviz path. render_g6(): AntV G6 alternative renderer.

title: gdalviz 0.1.0
text: render_command_line() round-trips pipelines canonical GDALG command lines; render_script() formats bash / PowerShell scripts (optionally reflowing SQL heredocs); as_gdalg() / write_gdalg() emit GDALG JSON specifications.
